name: License desktop manager (Python + MongoDB)
description: CustomTkinter desktop app for generating, searching, and exporting licenses with MongoDB storage—input validation at the UI, a clear persistence layer, and workstation-safe handling of connection secrets.
longDescription: A Python desktop utility for teams managing license records locally: create structured licenses, search and filter large sets, export to CSV, and keep MongoDB as the system of record with connection pooling. The UI emphasises form validation before writes, randomised sample data for testing without touching production, and automatic conversion of timestamps to Europe/Lisbon for operational clarity. A dedicated manager abstraction keeps Mongo concerns out of widget code so the architecture stays testable and easier to harden.
features: License generation and editing flows with validation before persistence
MongoDB-backed persistence layer with an explicit manager abstraction
Search and filter across license fields
Random data generator for demos and QA
highlights: Pragmatic desktop UI without requiring a web deployment—smaller attack surface than a public endpoint
Explicit Mongo models and manager abstraction—separation of concerns for data integrity
CSV exports designed to avoid leaking internal DB identifiers when shared externally
challenges: Desktop apps must guard database credentials on workstations and avoid leaking connection strings through screenshots or logs.
solutions: Use .env templates, keep secrets out of version control, and document secure handling for MongoDB URIs on workstations.
security: Secrets loaded from environment files—not committed defaults
Validate inputs at the UI layer before persistence
Export flows should exclude internal database identifiers when sharing CSVs externally
